summary: C++ compilation failed while building pandas SIMD code: explicit template instantiation for accumulate_moments_simd::operator() used xsimd::neon64 but the declared template signature uses xsimd::neon, causing the build to stop.
root_cause: The compiler reports explicit instantiation errors: an instantiation with xsimd::neon64 does not match the declared candidate template which uses xsimd::neon (neon vs neon64 mismatch) in the SIMD moments code, causing clang++ to fail compilation.

evidence.quote: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Shows the compiler emitted an explicit-instantiation error that caused the compile to fail.
The code attempts an explicit instantiation with xsimd::neon64 (the instantiation site).

evidence.quote: candidate template ignored: could not match 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int) noexcept' ... against 'Moments (xsimd::neon, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int)'
evidence.reason: Compiler note shows the declared candidate template uses xsimd::neon while the instantiation used xsimd::neon64, indicating a type/name mismatch.
Build target failed (compilation error) for the neon SIMD instantiation object file.
Confirms the build system stopped because the compiler subcommand failed.

suggested_fix: Make the template declaration and explicit instantiation use the same xsimd SIMD type. Concretely, update the code in ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp or ../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp so the extern template / template instantiation targets the same type (either xsimd::neon or xsimd::neon64) for this platform, or add conditional compilation to select the correct xsimd type for the macOS aarch64 build, then rebuild.
